title: The Tango Lesson
introKo:
동네 문화센터에서 배우는 아주 건전한 탱고 강습 이야기.
…표면적으로는요. 단어를 탭하면 속뜻이 보여요.

quiz.q: Carlos는 탱고가 두 몸이 나누는 무엇이라고 했나요?

normal.text: Friday evening. You walk into the community center for your first tango class. The teacher, Carlos, waits in the middle of the room.
"Welcome," he says. "Tango is a conversation between two bodies. Tonight, you will learn to listen."
He takes your hand. "First, the hold. Come closer. Closer. In tango, there is no polite distance."

options.en: Like this? Is this close enough?
options.reaction: "Closer," he says. "When you can feel your partner breathe, you are ready."

options.en: Okay, but I'm a little nervous.
options.reaction: "Good," he smiles. "The body dances better when the heart beats fast."

normal.text: "My hand goes here," he says, and places it on your waist. "Firm, but gentle. You must trust the hand that holds you."
"Now the lead. I lead, you follow. Do not think. Feel my body, and let yours answer."

options.en: Then lead me. I'll go wherever you take me.
options.ko: 그럼 리드해 줘요. 어디로 데려가든 따라갈게요.
options.reaction: "You will feel it," he says. "My body will tell yours. Words are not needed."

options.en: What if I make a mistake?
options.reaction: "In tango there are no mistakes," he says. "Only moves we did not plan."

normal.text: You walk together across the floor. Step, step, pause. Slowly, your two bodies find one rhythm.
"Now hook your leg around mine," Carlos says. "Slowly. Yes — let them tangle. Do not hurry out of it."
The music slows. Your face is warm. "Good," Carlos says. "A cold dancer makes a cold tango. Heat is part of the dance."

options.reaction: "Ah, the dip," he smiles. "Beginners always rush to the ending. The good ones learn to take their time."

options.en: I don't want this song to end.
options.reaction: "Everyone says that on the first night," he laughs. "Tango does that to people."

normal.text: For the last move, Carlos dips you low and holds you there. "Give me your weight. I have you. Stay — I could hold this all night, but the music, sadly, cannot."
You walk home with tired legs and a busy heart. Next week feels very far away. Once tango is in your body, it never leaves.

hard.text: It is Friday evening, and you push open the door of the community center, where your first tango class is about to begin. The teacher, Carlos, is waiting in the middle of the room.
"Welcome," he says with a small smile. "Tango is a conversation between two bodies. Tonight I will teach you how to listen."
He offers his hand, and you take it without thinking twice. "First, the hold. Come closer. Closer still. Tango is not a dance that respects polite distance."

options.en: Like this? Is this close enough for you?
options.reaction: "Closer," he says. "When you can feel your partner breathe, then you are ready to begin."

options.en: Okay, but I have to admit I'm a little nervous.
options.reaction: "Good," he smiles. "The body always dances better when the heart is beating fast."

hard.text: "My hand belongs here," he says, settling it on your waist. "Firm, but gentle. You must learn to trust the hand that holds you."
"Now the lead. I lead, and you follow. Do not think about the steps. Feel what my body is saying, and let yours answer."

options.en: Then lead me — I'll follow you wherever you decide to go.
options.ko: 그럼 리드해 줘요. 어디로 데려가든 따라갈게요.
options.reaction: "You will feel it," he says. "My body will tell yours everything. Words are rarely needed."

options.en: What happens if I make a mistake?
options.reaction: "In tango there are no mistakes," he says, "only moves that nobody planned."

hard.text: You move together across the floor. Step, step, pause. Little by little, your two bodies settle into a single rhythm.
"Now hook your leg around mine," Carlos says. "Slowly. Yes — let them tangle properly. Do not be in a hurry to untangle them."
The music slows, and you notice that your face is burning. "Good," Carlos says. "A cold dancer makes a cold tango. Heat is part of the dance."

options.en: Could we try the ending together?
options.reaction: "Ah, the dip," he smiles. "Beginners are always in a hurry to reach the ending. The good ones take their time."

options.en: Honestly, I don't want this song to end.
options.ko: 솔직히 이 노래가 안 끝났으면 좋겠어요.
options.reaction: "Everyone says that on their first night," he laughs. "Tango has that effect on people."

hard.text: For the final move, Carlos dips you low and holds you there. "Give me all your weight. I have you. Stay — I could hold this all night, but sadly, the music cannot."
You walk home on legs that no longer feel like yours, with a heart that refuses to slow down. Next week seems terribly far away. Once tango is in your body, it never leaves.

evening.en: the end part of the day

community.en: people who live in the same area

tango.en: a slow partner dance from Argentina

middle.en: the center point of a place

welcome.en: a friendly hello to a guest

between.en: in the space of two things

listen.en: to pay attention with your ears

breathe.en: to take air in and out

beats.en: moves in a rhythm, like a heart

beating.en: moving in a rhythm, like a heart

waist.en: the middle part of your body

follow.en: to move where the leader goes

do.en: a helper word for questions and not

steps.en: moves of the feet in dance

know.en: to have something in your mind

move.en: one action of the body in dance

across.en: from one side to the other

floor.en: the flat surface you dance on

hook.en: to curl one thing around another

leg.en: the body part you walk with

legs.en: the body parts you walk with

dip.en: a dance move bending your partner back

does.en: form of do for he or she

longer.ko: 더 오래 (no longer: 더 이상 ~ 않다)

offers.en: holds out for you to take

reach.en: to arrive at, to get to

exactly.en: in a precise way, just right

monday.en: the first work day of the week

practice.en: to do again and again to learn

private.en: for one person only, not shared

wink.hold: 탱고 기본 자세의 정식 명칭이에요. 수업 밖에서 이 단어는 그냥 '안는 것'이라, 어느 쪽 수업이었는지는 억양이 결정하죠.
wink.closer: '더 가까이'라는 춤 지시어예요. 반복해서 들으면 춤 얘기가 아닌 것처럼 들리기 시작하죠.
wink.lead: 춤에서 방향을 이끄는 역할이에요. 둘 사이의 주도권 얘기로도 읽힐 수 있고요.
wink.follow: 리드를 따라간다는 춤 용어예요. 몸을 상대에게 맡긴다는 뜻으로 들리면… 기분 탓일까요.
wink.waist: '허리에 손'은 탱고의 공식 기본 자세예요. 다만 'My hand belongs here'라는 말은, 문맥 없이 들으면 춤 설명처럼 안 들릴 뿐이죠.
wink.tangle: 다리가 엉키는 정식 탱고 스텝이에요. 문맥 없이 들으면 꽤 다른 장면이 떠오를 수 있죠.
wink.rhythm: 두 사람이 하나의 박자를 탄다는 춤 용어예요. 'We found our rhythm'은 춤 밖에서도 쓰는 표현인데, 어디서 쓰는지는 각자 아는 만큼만 떠올리기로 해요.
wink.dip: 상대를 뒤로 깊게 눕히는 마무리 동작이에요. '무게를 다 맡겨요'라는 말과 만나면, 상상은 각자의 몫이에요.
wink.dips: 상대를 뒤로 깊게 눕히는 마무리 동작이에요. '무게를 다 맡겨요'라는 말과 만나면, 상상은 각자의 몫이에요.

winkEn.hold: The official name for tango's basic embrace. Outside class, holding someone this close usually needs a different explanation.
winkEn.closer: A standard dance instruction. Repeat it enough times in a low voice and it stops sounding like one.
winkEn.lead: The dancer who decides where things go. Also a fair question about who is steering this whole evening.
winkEn.follow: Dance term for trusting the lead completely. Letting someone else move your body is, of course, strictly technical here.
winkEn.waist: Hand on the waist is textbook tango form. 'My hand belongs here,' heard out of context, is a much bolder claim.
winkEn.tangle: A legitimate tango step where legs intertwine. Described over the phone, it would raise a few questions.
winkEn.rhythm: Two people finding one beat — pure dance vocabulary. 'We found our rhythm' travels well beyond the dance floor, though.
winkEn.dip: The closing move where you lean back and give someone all your weight. Paired with 'I could hold this all night,' it does a lot of work.
winkEn.dips: The closing move where you lean back and give someone all your weight. Paired with 'I could hold this all night,' it does a lot of work.

introEn:
A perfectly wholesome tango lesson at the community center.
...on the surface. Tap words to see the hidden meaning.

0.name: 신중한 무용수
0.en: At home, you practice the steps alone, with one hand on a kitchen chair. Slow, careful, no hurry — exactly how you like it. The chair is a patient partner: it never asks you to come closer.
0.ko: 집에 돌아와 부엌 의자에 한 손을 얹고 혼자 스텝을 연습해요. 천천히, 조심조심, 서두르지 않고 — 딱 좋아하는 방식대로요. 의자는 참을성 있는 파트너예요. 더 가까이 오라는 말은 절대 안 하거든요.

1.name: 설레는 초보자
1.en: All week, you secretly count the steps under your desk at work. On Friday you arrive early, but you wait outside until another student goes in first. Carlos sees you and smiles — some conversations are worth being a little nervous for.
1.ko: 일주일 내내 회사 책상 밑에서 몰래 발로 스텝을 밟아 봐요. 금요일엔 일찍 도착하지만, 다른 수강생이 먼저 들어갈 때까지 밖에서 기다려요. 카를로스가 당신을 보고 미소 지어요 — 어떤 대화는 조금 긴장되더라도 해 볼 만하니까요.

2.name: 망설임 없는 파트너
2.en: On Monday you buy real dance shoes, and on Friday you are the first one through the door. When Carlos offers his hand, you take it before he finishes the question. 'You learn fast,' he says — but you already knew that.
2.ko: 월요일에 진짜 댄스화를 사고, 금요일엔 제일 먼저 문을 열고 들어가요. 카를로스가 손을 내밀자 질문이 끝나기도 전에 잡아요. '빨리 배우시네요.' 그가 말하지만 — 그건 이미 알고 있었어요.

3.name: 플로어의 불꽃
3.en: The next Friday, you ask Carlos for private lessons, because the class feels too short and you have so much to learn. He looks at you for a long moment and says, 'Then we will need many, many nights.' You were hoping he would say that.
3.ko: 다음 금요일, 카를로스에게 개인 레슨을 부탁해요. 수업이 너무 짧고, 배울 게 아직 너무 많거든요. 그가 당신을 한참 바라보다가 말해요. '그럼 밤이 아주, 아주 많이 필요하겠네요.' 사실 그 말을 기다리고 있었어요.
